data.title: SF Holding Logs 6% Rise in February Revenue
data.description:
SF Holding posted a total revenue of 18.4 billion yuan in February, up 6.4% from the previous year.

Express logistics revenue rose 3.8% year over year to 13.1 billion yuan and supply chain and international revenue climbed 14% to 5.22 billion yuan, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Top Glove: Competition Could Be Mitigated by Diversified Product Portfolio
Tesoro Gold Intersects Shallow Gold Mineralization at Chile Gold Project
data.description:
Tesoro Gold said that the results of an extensional and infill drilling program at the El Zorro gold project in Chile intersected a wide, shallow zone of gold mineralization, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Assay results from 14 diamond holes drilled returned intercepts of 68.23 meters at 1.18 grams per tonne grade of gold from a depth of 32.77 meters as well as 26.97 meters at 2 g/t grade of gold from 105.25 meters.

It said that drilling is ongoing, with a focus on mineral resource estimate expansion at the northern and southern ends of the Ternera deposit.

data.title: ReFuels to Form Clean Fuel Company with Backing from Foresight-managed Funds
data.description:
ReFuels secured backing from Foresight Group-managed funds for its plan to create a fully integrated clean fuel company, according to a Thursday release.

The clean fuel company will be created through the European renewable biomethane supplier's subsidiary CNG Fuels. The unit will receive 15 compressed natural gas stations and mobile refueling equipment from Foresight as part of the plan.

Completion of the transaction is expected in April, subject to certain closing conditions, including the approval of ReFuels' shareholders during an extraordinary general meeting slated for April 7.

Following the transaction, Foresight-managed funds will own 60% of CNG Fuels, with ReFuels holding the remaining 40%. The deal will give the business the capacity to source, generate, and transport biomethane, as well as have direct station ownership, a large site pipeline, and market-leading station operating capabilities.

Based on their ownership of the business after the deal, Foresight and ReFuels are entitled to 10% shareholder loan instruments of 150.2 million pounds sterling and 16 million pounds, respectively. Over three years and subject to certain milestones, the companies may also receive up to an additional 18 million pounds in distribution from CNG Fuels.

data.title: OPEC, IEA focus on China oil demand, but crude imports are what matter: Russell
data.description:
What's more important for crude oil markets? Forecasts of Chinese oil demand growth by leading agencies or the reality of ongoing weakness in imports?

Both the International Energy Agency (IEA) and the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) talk about demand when they make forecasts as to the state of the oil sector in China, the world's biggest crude importer.

This is a long-standing practice and one that is largely unchallenged by the wider market.

The problem is that there is a disconnect between the forecasts of demand growth amounting to several hundred thousand barrels per day (bpd) and the reality that China's oil imports are actually falling.

In its latest monthly market report OPEC said that China's oil demand was 16.68 million bpd in 2024, a rise of 320,000 bpd from the 16.36 million bpd the exporter group said it was in 2023.

However, China's oil imports were 11.04 million bpd in 2024, according to customs data, which was down 2.1%, or 210,000 bpd from 2023.

Obviously demand includes more than just crude imports, as it also counts imports of products, domestic output and any changes in inventories.

But the gap between the bullish demand number from OPEC and the weakness in imports can't be bridged by any of the other components of demand.

China's imports of refined products were 1.05 million bpd in 2024, a figure calculated by using the customs figure of 48.23 million metric tons and converting at the BP Plc rate of eight barrels of product per ton.

Product imports were also down by 1.0% in 2024 from the previous year, according to the customs figures.

China's domestic crude production was 4.24 million bpd, up 1.4%, or 60,000 bpd from the 4.18 million bpd in 2023.

China doesn't disclose changes in commercial or strategic oil inventories, but an estimate of surplus crude can be made by subtracting what refiners processed from the total volume of crude available from imports and domestic output.

On this basis China added about 1.15 million bpd to stockpiles in 2024, up from 760,000 bpd in 2023.

Putting the available data together shows that China's imports were weak in 2024, domestic production rose modestly and it's likely that the country was adding to crude inventories.

But the more important conclusion is that looking at forecasts for China's oil demand is likely to give a misleading picture of the actual situation.

PRICE LINK

If OPEC's gain of 320,000 bpd in China's oil demand in 2024 is the right metric to use, it would follow that Chinese demand would be bullish for crude prices.

But crude prices struggled last year, with global benchmark Brent futures dropping from a year high of $91.05 a barrel on April 15 to as low as $68.68 by Sept. 10, before ending 2024 at $74.64.

While China is far from the sole driver of the crude price, it does play an outsized role given it accounts for about one-quarter of global seaborne imports, and it's the seaborne price that drives the entire crude market.

The IEA was more modest than OPEC in its China numbers, with its March monthly report pegging total oil product demand growth at 151,000 bpd in 2024, while forecasting a gain of 228,000 bpd in 2025.

OPEC's latest report forecasts that China's oil demand will rise by 310,000 bpd in 2025, more optimistic than the IEA's estimate.

But once again, the reality of weak crude imports is continuing, with customs data showing arrivals of 10.42 million bpd in the first two months of 2025, down 370,000 bpd, or 3.4%, from the 10.79 million bpd recorded for the same period in 2024.

It's possible that lower crude prices and a recovery in economic momentum could see China's crude imports recover over the rest of 2025.

But for now, the forecasts for oil demand growth in China being made by OPEC and the IEA are already at odds with the declining imports.

The views expressed here are those of the author, a columnist for Reuters.

data.title: Top Glove: Expect Continued Build-Up in Order Book Amid Strong Order Inflows
XAU/USD: Gold Blasts Off to Record of $3,060 as Powell Floats Two Cuts Amid Slower Growth
data.description:
Key points:
  • Gold prices rally to new all-time high
  • Gold bugs cheer Powell’s message
  • Immediate resistance at $3,070

Fed’s rate-setting meeting wrapped up with a Powell presser that supported the bullish narrative for gold. Here’s what’s happening.

🎁 Gold Sets New Record

  • Gold prices rallied hard late Wednesday and remained well-bid early Thursday. The precious metal confidently charted the rarefied air above $3,000, breaking its previous record high and set a new one at $3,060 an ounce. This time it wasn’t just tariffs and war tremors — it was Powell’s message.
  • The Federal Reserve kept a steady hand on interest rates with no changes in line with expectations. What wasn’t expected was Fed boss Jay Powell’s reassuring stance on the central bank’s forward guidance.

✅ Gold Rush Activated

  • And that’s what triggered the gold rush. Speaking at the usual post-decision press conference, Powell said officials were leaning toward two cuts to borrowing costs this year, maintaining their previous point of view for the rate path. What about Trump’s tariffs? There’s (mostly) no reason to worry, Powell hinted.
  • The two cuts to borrowing costs this year are expected to bolster economic growth, which is likely to struggle, according to the Fed. Donald Trump’s tariffs, Powell said, are inflationary and can hinder progress made in the economy, both on the business and the consumer side.

🤫 Resistance at $3,070

  • Gold bugs liked it — lower rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding the precious metal as it doesn’t generate yield or pay dividends. When the Fed trims rates, it diminishes the appeal of fixed-income assets (like bonds) and investors pivot to safe-haven alternatives — “Hi, gold.”
  • In this light, bullion’s upside swing is staring at the immediate resistance of $3,070 — a level at which the price is expected to either bounce off or break through since it’s the upper end of a long-term ascending channel.

data.title: Top Glove: Trade Rerouting From U.S. Tariffs Could Lead to Higher Plant Utilization Rates and Stronger Selling Prices
Geely Automobile's Net More Than Triples on Record Exports, Robust Sales
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Geely Automobile's net profit in 2024 more than tripled on robust sales and record exports, helping cement the company as one of the leading players in China's intensely competitive EV sector.

The Chinese automaker said Thursday that its 2024 net profit surged 213% to 16.63 billion yuan, equivalent to $2.30 billion, as revenue rose 34% to 240.19 billion yuan.

The robust earnings were largely driven by an outstanding performance in the overseas market and solid sales at home. Its total export wholesale volume rose 57% to 414,522 units, a new record high.

Improved economies of scale, a better product mix and cost controls helped its 2025 gross profit margin rise 0.6 percentage point to 15.9%, the company said.

Geely in February merged its two units --Zeekr and Lynk&Co--into Zeekr Group in order to better compete in the market with an eye to overpass its international rivals within the next two years.

Zeekr's total vehicle deliveries last year surged 87% to 222,123, helping narrow its net loss to 6.42 billion yuan from 8.35 billion yuan in 2023.

Geely said it expects the demand for vehicles will remain stable this year, though it noted that competition within the auto industry would intensifying in terms of both technology and pricing.

Geely set its 2025 sales target at 2.71 million units, up 25% from last year.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Green Packet Prices Second Tranche of Private Placement Shares
data.description:
Green Packet said it fixed the price of the second tranche of its private share placement shares at 0.0264 ringgit apiece.

The price represents a discount of 19.76% to the five-day volume-weighted average market price of shares up to March 17, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

The Malaysia-based international technology company plans to issue about 30% of the total number of issued shares, according to earleir filings.

data.title: Market Chatter: TotalEnergies Considering Green Hydrogen Imports from Brazilian Project
data.description:
TotalEnergies is looking to import green hydrogen from a Brazilian for its European refineries, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The oil and gas giant is also considering a direct stake in the multi-billion dollar project being developed in northeastern Brazil by Casa dos Ventos, according to the sources. No final decision has been made yet.

TotalEnergies did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Top Glove: Remains Vigilant to Competitive Pressures, Particularly in Non U.S. Markets Amid China's Price War
BRICS, planes and beef in spotlight as Brazil's Lula visits Vietnam, sources say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lula to meet Vietnamese leaders in Hanoi to boost ties
  • Business delegation with Lula includes Embraer, JBS
  • JBS eyes meat plant if Brazil beef import approved, sources say
  • Embraer in talks to sell planes to Vietnam, sources say

Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva will visit Vietnam next week, bringing with him a business delegation including executives from planemaker Embraer and food giant JBS that are both in talks for possible deals in the Southeast Asian country, sources said.

Lula's second visit to Vietnam as president will take place as Vietnam, under pressure from the Trump administration to reduce its large trade surplus, is pledging to boost imports from the United States, including of farm products such as soybeans of which Brazil is a top exporter to the country.

Lula will travel to Vietnam on March 27-29 after visiting Japan, according to the Brazilian government.

Lula is expected to invite Vietnam to attend a BRICS summit in Brazil in July, a Brazilian official said, noting Vietnam was invited last year to become a BRICS partner but has so far not taken an official position on the matter.

Vietnam's foreign ministry did not reply to a request for comment. Brazil's embassy to Vietnam declined to comment.

The two countries are expected to agree on an action plan on defence, agriculture and energy, which could boost cooperation on ethanol, a fuel of which Brazil is a major global producer, the Brazilian official said.

Brazil wants also to increase exports to Vietnam and is asking Hanoi to authorise imports of its beef, the official said confirming earlier reports on Vietnamese state media.

BUSINESS TALKS

The opening of the Vietnamese market for Brazilian beef is a precondition for an investment that Brazil's food giant JBS is considering in Vietnam, three people briefed about the talks, including the Brazilian official, told Reuters.

The company is studying building a meat-processing hub in northern Vietnam, its first plant in Asia, with a possible investment of tens of millions of dollars, the three people said, declining to be named because the information was not public.

JBS declined to comment.

Separately, Embraer is also in talks for the possible sale of ten E190 narrow-body jets to flag carrier Vietnam Airlines, the Brazilian official said.

It is also trying to sell C-390 military transport planes, with a possible showcase flight in Vietnam in May, the official and an industry source said.

Embraer declined to comment. Vietnam Airlines did not reply to a request for comment.

Hanoi is in talks with the U.S. to buy C-130 military transport planes produced by Lockheed Martin .

Vietnam is one of the world's fastest-growing aviation markets and local carriers have long been seeking to expand their fleets.

In recent days senior executives from aviation giants Airbus and Boeing met top Vietnamese officials, according to state media and an internal agenda seen by Reuters. Vietnam is also working on the approval of China's COMAC jets.

data.title: Ancom Logistics' CEO Resigns
data.description:
Ancom Logistics chief executive officer Hasnul Bin Hassan stepped down on Wednesday, according to a same-day Malaysian bourse filing.

The resignation is in line with parent company Ancom Nylex's management restructuring, Hasnul Bin Hassan will focus more on the other business of Ancom Nylex.

EMEA Morning Briefing: Markets Await Central Bank Decisions
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Bank of England Rate Decision, U.K. unemployment; trading updates for Hapag-Lloyd, Investec, RWE

Opening Call:

European stock futures were broadly lower after Asia stocks traded mixed. U.S. Treasury yields were lower, the dollar slightly weakened, while gold rose and oil edged higher.

Equities:

European futures pointed to a mostly lower open as investors further digested the Federal Reserve's decision to extend its pause on interest rate cuts. Investors looked ahead to the meetings of Bank of England, Swiss National Bank and Sweden's Riksbank later in the day.

The BOE is expected to leave rates on hold and continue to signal that cuts in the future will be gradual.

Economists at Nomura led by George Buckley said, "We expect the bank to remain on hold in March, before cutting again in May," and to then cut at every other meeting "between now and February 2026, inclusive, for a terminal rate of 3.5%."

Markets are expected to have a muted reaction to the central bank's decision, Danske Bank analysts said, reiterating its expectations for the BOE to maintain its guidance of gradual and cautious interest-rate cuts.

Forex:

The Bank of England's decision is unlikely to have much influence on sterling as markets have already priced in prospects of interest rates being left on hold at 4.50%, said TD Securities analysts.

Instead, sterling will probably be influenced by global developments rather than domestic factors, they said.

The BOE's guidance is "likely to remain unchanged and highlight data dependence, with the wording pointing towards continuing 'gradual' cuts, " the analysts added.

Bonds:

Market expectations over limited interest-rate cuts by the Bank of England is keeping gilt yields higher than their developed markets peers, said MUFG's Lee Hardman, adding that inflation concerns are likely to limit the pace of rate cuts.

"It will become more uncomfortable for the BOE to keep cutting rates heading into the summer when inflation is expected to temporarily pick up towards 4.0%," he said.

-

Treasury yields fell overnight as the Fed's moves broadly met expectations, with interest rates being held while officials' projection for inflation ticked up and growth forecast declined.

Estimates of two interest rate cuts this year stayed unchanged, in line with markets pricing.

Energy:

Oil edged up amid an uncertain outlook for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, with Russia and Ukraine exchanging allegations that each side had attacked energy and oil facilities overnight.

This development came just hours after Russian President Putin agreed to a 30-day pause in attacks on energy infrastructure and Ukrainian President Zelensky also agreed to the partial cease-fire against power and energy facilities, the White House said.

"The oil market has experienced volatility in recent days, facing conflicting forces that keep traders under uncertainty," said Pepperstone research strategist Quasar Elizundia.

Metals:

Gold rose after its front-month futures set a new record high ahead of the Fed's rate decision, marking seven consecutive sessions that gold has finished higher.

Going forward for the precious metals prices, what may counterweigh the Fed's inflation uncertainty is if the peace negotiations in the Russia-Ukraine war yield a positive result, analysts said.

Safe haven demand has been the driving force for gold prices in recent sessions, they added.

-

Copper rose, breaking above the psychological threshhold of $10,000 a ton as investors braced for additional tariff announcements in the coming days.

"In the long term, tariffs could be bearish for copper and other industrial metals in the context of slowing growth and keeping inflation higher for longer," ING analysts wrote.

"With growth in the U.S. likely to slow on the back of tariffs and China already struggling to revive its economy, demand for copper and other industrial metals is likely to weaken looking ahead," they said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Fed Projections See an Economy Dramatically Reset by Trump's Election

The Federal Reserve's first set of projections since Donald Trump's inauguration underscored-in the central bank's understated and technocratic fashion-just how much the president's plans to press ahead with widespread tariffs have turned the economic outlook on its head.

Months ago, policymakers presumed they would spend 2025 gradually cutting rates to keep inflation heading down without a big rise in joblessness to achieve the so-called soft landing. The latest projections point to the prospect that tariffs covering a swath of goods and materials will send up prices while sapping investment, sentiment and growth, at least in the short run.

U.K. Is Expected to Follow Fed With Rate Pause. Why the BOE Really Wants to Cut.

The Bank of England is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged this week, in line with the Federal Reserve's action on Wednesday. It is still likely to cut rates more than the Fed later this year.

Like its U.S. counterpart, the U.K. central bank expects inflation to continue to slow over the next few months, but it wants to be sure it is on track and avoid lowering borrowing costs too quickly.

How Trump's talk with Putin about Ukraine involves this top U.S. industry

U.S. President Donald Trump bragged earlier this week that gasoline prices are down since he took office in January - proof, the White House claims, that his economic policies are working. A more accurate reason could be that the world's two biggest economies - America and China - are both slowing, thus lowering demand and pushing global prices down.

Russia Jailed Her Husband In Occupied Ukraine. Then Natalya Swung Into Action.

Natalya Barchuk edged nervously toward the prison in the Russian-occupied city of Kalanchak in southern Ukraine clutching a parcel of food and a bag of fresh clothes.

She'd been told her husband might be inside, one of thousands of civilians detained in an opaque prison system on occupied territory under the ultimate control of the Russian security service. Guards accepted the care packages but wouldn't tell her whether her Oleksiy was there.

EU Pushes Ahead With Big Tech Antitrust Enforcement

The European Commission moved forward with the enforcement of its digital antitrust rules on Apple and Alphabet's Google amid rising trade tensions with the U.S. over the treatment of American tech giants.

The commission said Wednesday that it is concerned that Google's search results display-and its terms for app developers using its Play Store on Android phones-break the rules of the Digital Markets Act by giving Google's own tech an unfair advantage over others.

SoftBank to Buy Semiconductor Company Ampere Computing for $6.5 Billion

SoftBank Group inked a deal to purchase Ampere Computing, a fabless semiconductor company, for $6.5 billion in cash.

Japanese investment holding company SoftBank said Wednesday that the purchase would help to enhance its capabilities in key artificial-intelligence areas and accelerate its growth initiatives. The deal comes as the company is broadening its AI-infrastructure investments.

Bondi's Campaign Against Domestic Terrorism Starts With Tesla Foes

Teslas set on fire. Bullets and bombs aimed at showrooms and charging stations. Is it terrorism?

Attorney General Pam Bondi thinks so, declaring a Justice Department crackdown on what she views as politically motivated attacks against Tesla leader Elon Musk, one of President Trump's most powerful advisers.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com
